chapter_summary: Chapter 5 begins with a call to listen carefully to wisdom and think before acting. It warns that some things can look sweet and wonderful on the outside but lead to bitter harm. Stay close to God's ways, stay close to your family, keep your promises, and be careful about the wrong kinds of friendships. The chapter ends with an important reminder: God sees everything we do. Nothing is hidden from Him. When we remember that God is always watching — not to catch us doing wrong, but because He loves us — it helps us choose what is right.
main_lesson: God sees everything you do. Choose what is right because God loves you and is always with you.
memory_verse: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
parent_guide: Proverbs 5 addresses warnings about relationships that dishonour God (vv.3-20) and closes with a powerful theological statement about God's omniscience and the consequences of sin (vv.21-23). For children, the chapter's core message is: some things look sweet but lead to bitter harm; stay loyal to your family and God's good ways; and remember God sees everything because He loves you. Verses 15-20 address marital faithfulness; in children's adaptations these are presented as keeping promises and being faithful to family. Parents may choose to read and discuss these verses together at an appropriate level. The closing verses (21-23) are rich and fully accessible to young children.
application_for_children: Practise asking yourself this week: 'Would I do this if God were standing right next to me?' — because He is. When you choose to do right even when no one is watching, you are living wisely.

verses.kjv: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
verses.little_bible: My child, pay close attention to wisdom. Lean in and listen carefully to understanding.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lean in and listen to wisdom.
verses.meaning: Real wisdom requires our full attention — not half-listening, but leaning in with our whole mind and heart.
verses.memory_phrase: I lean in and listen to wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me pay full attention when You or wise people are teaching me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it look like to really, truly listen — not just hear?
verses.family_discussion: How can we become a family that really listens — to God and to each other?
verses.do_it_today: When someone is speaking to you today, put everything else down and look at them. Really listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child leaning forward with wide, attentive eyes and a gentle smile, listening carefully to a wise teacher

verses.kjv: That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
verses.little_bible: Think before you act, and say only what is wise and good.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Think carefully before you speak or act.
verses.meaning: Wisdom means pausing before we act or speak — checking our heart and mind before our words or actions go out.
verses.memory_phrase: I think before I speak or act.
verses.prayer: God, help me pause and think before I speak or do anything today.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you say something without thinking first?
verses.family_discussion: Tell about a time someone's kind, thoughtful words made a big difference.
verses.do_it_today: Before you say something today, ask yourself: 'Is it true? Is it kind? Is it helpful?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing with a finger on their chin, thinking carefully before speaking, with gentle light around them

verses.kjv: For the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb, and her mouth is smoother than oil:
verses.little_bible: Some people's words are as sweet as honey and as smooth as oil — they sound so good at first.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some words sound sweet, but may not be safe.
verses.meaning: Not everything that sounds pleasant is good for us. Sweet-sounding words can hide dangerous intentions. Wisdom helps us test what we hear.
verses.memory_phrase: Not everything sweet is safe.
verses.prayer: God, help me be wise about the words I hear, even when they sound very sweet.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever tasted something that looked delicious but was not good for you?
verses.family_discussion: How can we tell the difference between words that are truly good and words that just sound good?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent: 'Can you help me practise telling good words from tricky ones?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A honeycomb dripping golden honey, but with a subtle shadow behind it — beauty that needs wisdom to test

verses.kjv: But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
verses.little_bible: But the ending is bitter and painful — like swallowing something horribly sour, or a blade that cuts both ways.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Things that seem sweet but lead away from God end in pain.
verses.meaning: Sweet beginnings on the wrong path lead to bitter ends. What seems attractive can turn sharp and harmful when it leads away from God's ways.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong paths end in bitterness.
verses.prayer: God, help me see past sweet beginnings to where a wrong path truly leads.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever started something that seemed great but ended badly? What did you learn?
verses.family_discussion: How does wisdom help us see where a path will end, not just where it begins?
verses.do_it_today: Before starting something today, ask: 'Where will this lead? Will it end well?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A honey-sweet beginning turning into a bitter fruit further down a winding path — wisdom showing both ends

verses.kjv: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.
verses.little_bible: The path of going the wrong way leads downward — away from life, away from God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wrong path leads down and away from God.
verses.meaning: Persistent wrong choices ultimately lead away from God and toward spiritual death. This solemn warning underlines how serious wrong paths are.
verses.memory_phrase: God's path leads to life.
verses.prayer: God, keep my feet on the path that leads to life — Your path.
verses.discussion_question: Which direction do you want your life to go — up toward God or down away from Him?
verses.family_discussion: How does staying close to God keep our life going in the right direction?
verses.do_it_today: Draw two arrows — one pointing up toward God, one pointing down. Think about which direction your choices are pointing today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright upward path toward light on one side, a dim downward path on the other — a child choosing up

verses.kjv: Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them.
verses.little_bible: Wrong ways are shifting and hard to pin down — they keep moving so you cannot quite see where they lead. That is how they trap people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong paths are tricky and keep changing so you cannot see where they go.
verses.meaning: One of the deceptions of wrong paths is that they are unclear — they shift and change so we cannot easily trace their full danger until it is too late.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way is clear and steady.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your path is clear and steady, not shifting and tricky.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been somewhere confusing where the paths kept changing? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: How does staying in God's Word keep our path clear when the world around us is confusing?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse that shows you clearly the right way to go today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A shifting, foggy maze of paths on one side — and a clear, glowing straight road on the other

verses.kjv: Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.
verses.little_bible: Children, listen to me right now. Do not go away from the words I am telling you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Listen right now. Stay close to these good words.
verses.meaning: The call is urgent and direct — these warnings are for children who need to hear them now, while they are forming their habits and character.
verses.memory_phrase: I listen right now.
verses.prayer: God, help me hear Your words right now and keep them close.
verses.discussion_question: Why does the verse say 'hear me NOW' — why is it important to listen right away?
verses.family_discussion: Is there wisdom your family has been putting off taking seriously? Now is the time.
verses.do_it_today: Choose one wise thing you have been meaning to start — begin it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of children sitting in a circle, leaning in, listening eagerly to a wise person speaking

verses.kjv: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
verses.little_bible: Stay far away from the wrong path. Do not even go near the place where wrong things happen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Stay far away from wrong places.
verses.meaning: The best protection is distance. Wisdom says: do not get close to danger — stay well away from where wrong things begin.
verses.memory_phrase: I stay far away from wrong.
verses.prayer: God, help me stay far from wrong places and wrong situations.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it wiser to stay away from trouble rather than getting close and then trying to stop?
verses.family_discussion: What places or situations should our family stay well away from?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one place or situation you should keep distance from — and make a plan to stay away.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking a wide berth around a dark, shadowy house, staying on the bright safe path far away

verses.kjv: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:
verses.little_bible: Otherwise you might give your good name and your precious years of life to people who are cruel and do not care about you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong friendships can take away your honour and your best years.
verses.meaning: Wrong relationships can cost us our honour and our most precious years. What we give our time and loyalty to shapes what becomes of us.
verses.memory_phrase: I give my honour and time to what is right.
verses.prayer: God, help me give my honour and my best years to things and people that are truly good.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when someone spends all their time with people who do not care about them?
verses.family_discussion: Who are the people in your life who truly care about your honour and your future?
verses.do_it_today: Thank someone today who has protected your honour and spoken good about you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child being protected by a caring family, their honour like a gentle glow around them, safe from harm

verses.kjv: Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
verses.little_bible: Otherwise everything you work hard for might end up going to strangers who do not deserve it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices can mean all your hard work goes to waste.
verses.meaning: Our hard work is precious. Poor choices can lead to our efforts and resources benefiting those who have no care for us.
verses.memory_phrase: I work hard for what is right.
verses.prayer: God, help me use my hard work and effort for things that truly honour You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever worked really hard on something only for it to be taken away? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: How do wise choices protect the things we work hard for?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing you are working hard for — make sure it is for something truly good.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child's careful handiwork — a painting or model — glowing with their effort, being kept safely by a loving family

verses.kjv: And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
verses.little_bible: And in the end, you will grieve — when all the wrong choices have worn you out and caused you deep sorrow.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices bring sadness at the end.
verses.meaning: The ultimate consequence of persisting in wrong ways is grief and exhaustion. The warning here is full of compassion — God wants to spare us this ending.
verses.memory_phrase: Right choices today prevent sadness later.
verses.prayer: God, help me make right choices now so I do not face sadness and regret later.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it better to choose right now than to be sorry later?
verses.family_discussion: How does thinking about where our choices lead help us make better ones now?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a right choice you can make now that will spare you sadness later. Make it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two roads: one that starts easy but ends in tears, one that starts with effort but ends in bright joy

verses.kjv: And say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;
verses.little_bible: And you will say: 'Why did I hate being taught? Why did my heart push away correction?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: One day you might wish you had listened to correction sooner.
verses.meaning: The person who refused wisdom and correction looks back with regret. Receiving correction willingly now saves great sorrow later.
verses.memory_phrase: I welcome correction today.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that welcomes correction and does not push it away.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever NOT listened to good advice and later wished you had?
verses.family_discussion: Why do people sometimes push away correction — and what can we do to stay open to it?
verses.do_it_today: If someone corrects you today, take a deep breath, listen, and say 'Thank you.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a soft, open face receiving gentle correction from a caring adult — a future contrast to regret

verses.kjv: And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, and inclined not mine ear to them that instructed me!
verses.little_bible: And you will cry: 'I did not listen to my teachers! I did not lean my ear toward those who were trying to help me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Refusing to listen to teachers leads to sadness later.
verses.meaning: Looking back in regret at ignored instruction is one of life's bitterest experiences. Hearing and honouring those who teach us is wisdom in action.
verses.memory_phrase: I listen to those who teach me.
verses.prayer: God, help me listen to and honour every wise person who teaches me.
verses.discussion_question: Who are your teachers — at home, at school, at church? Do you really listen to them?
verses.family_discussion: How can we show honour and attention to those who are trying to teach us?
verses.do_it_today: Write a note of thanks to a teacher or someone who has taught you well.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gratefully thanking a teacher, both of them smiling, a warm and lasting moment of passing wisdom

verses.kjv: I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
verses.little_bible: Ignoring wisdom almost led me into every kind of wrong — right in front of everyone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Not listening to wisdom can lead to all kinds of wrong choices.
verses.meaning: A life of ignored wisdom can spiral into public disgrace. The warning here is complete — this is where the road of foolishness ends.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom keeps me from going wrong.
verses.prayer: God, let wisdom keep me from every kind of wrong. Protect me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean for something to 'spiral' — to keep getting bigger and worse?
verses.family_discussion: How do small wise choices protect us from big public disasters?
verses.do_it_today: Make one small wise choice today — those are the building blocks that keep us from disaster.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child safely sheltered under God's protective light, surrounded by wisdom on all sides

verses.kjv: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.
verses.little_bible: Be satisfied with what God has given you. Drink from the good things right in your own home.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Be happy and thankful with what God has given you at home.
verses.meaning: God calls us to find contentment, satisfaction, and joy in what He has given us — especially in our family and home — rather than chasing what belongs to others.
verses.memory_phrase: I am happy and thankful for what God gives me.
verses.prayer: God, help me be truly happy and thankful for the good things You have given me and my family.
verses.discussion_question: What are the good things God has given you right at home?
verses.family_discussion: How can our family practise being content and thankful for what we already have?
verses.do_it_today: Name five good things God has given your family — thank Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child joyfully drinking from a bright well at home, grateful and satisfied, with a warm loving family around them

verses.kjv: Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad, and rivers of waters in the streets.
verses.little_bible: Let the blessings from your home overflow — let goodness flow out to others around you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Let the goodness from your home bless others around you.
verses.meaning: The blessing and goodness that God gives our family is not just for us to keep — it is meant to flow out and bless our wider community.
verses.memory_phrase: Our family's goodness flows out to others.
verses.prayer: God, let the blessings You give our family flow out and bless others around us.
verses.discussion_question: How can your family be a blessing to others in your neighbourhood or community?
verses.family_discussion: What gifts, skills, or goodness has God given your family to share with the world?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing that lets your family's goodness flow out to someone else today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A fountain from a happy home with water streaming out into the neighbourhood, blessing everyone around it

verses.kjv: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers' with thee.
verses.little_bible: What is most precious in your home — your family and their love — is yours. Guard it and keep it close.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep your family close and protect what is precious.
verses.meaning: The gifts and blessings of family are to be protected and kept close, not spread thin or shared carelessly with those outside.
verses.memory_phrase: I protect what is precious in my family.
verses.prayer: God, help our family protect and treasure each other.
verses.discussion_question: What in your family is most precious to you?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family guard and protect the love and closeness you share?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a family member what you treasure most about them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A family gathered closely together in a warm glowing home, arms around each other, safe and beloved

verses.kjv: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
verses.little_bible: God wants families to be full of joy. Be happy with the family He gives you, and keep your promises to them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God wants your family to be happy. Keep your promises.
verses.meaning: God's design for family is joy, faithfulness, and blessing. Rejoicing in and being faithful to those God has given us is His good plan.
verses.memory_phrase: God wants my family to be full of joy.
verses.prayer: God, fill our family with joy. Help us keep our promises and love each other well.
verses.discussion_question: What makes your family the happiest?
verses.family_discussion: What promises has your family made to each other? How are you keeping them?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing that brings extra joy to your family today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful family laughing together at a bright sunny table, full of warmth and happiness

verses.kjv: Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.
verses.little_bible: God wants families to be filled with tenderness, closeness, and love — always satisfied in one another and faithful to each other.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God wants families to be close and full of love.
verses.meaning: God designed families to be a place of deep tenderness, closeness, and faithful love. Being completely devoted to your family is His good and holy plan.
verses.memory_phrase: Our family is full of love and tenderness.
verses.prayer: God, fill our family with closeness and tender love.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like when your family is really close and loving?
verses.family_discussion: How can we protect and deepen the closeness and love in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Give every family member a hug and tell them you love them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gentle, tender family scene — parents and children close together, warm and safe, a picture of loving faithfulness

verses.kjv: And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?
verses.little_bible: Why would you go looking for love in wrong places when God has given you a family to love? Stay loyal to those God has given you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Stay loyal to your family. Love those God has given you.
verses.meaning: God asks why we would chase what is wrong when He has already given us so much that is good. Loyalty to family and to God's ways is always the better choice.
verses.memory_phrase: I stay loyal to my family.
verses.prayer: God, help me be loyal to my family and not chase after wrong things.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it better to treasure the family God has given you than to look for something else?
verses.family_discussion: How do we practise loyalty to each other in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today that shows your loyalty and love to your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child choosing to go home to their family rather than wandering away, arms open, welcomed lovingly

verses.kjv: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
verses.little_bible: God sees every step you take. He watches over your whole life and knows everything you do.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God sees everything you do. He is always watching over you.
verses.meaning: Nothing is hidden from God. He sees every thought, word, and action — and because He loves us, He cares about all of them.
verses.memory_phrase: God sees everything I do.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember You are always with me and always watching over me.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that God can see everything — even the secret things?
verses.family_discussion: How is 'God sees everything' a comfort, not just a warning?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good, kind thing today that no one else will see — just you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child doing something kind quietly in a corner, with God's gentle eyes watching warmly from above

verses.kjv: His own iniquities shall take the wicked, and he shall be holden with the cords of his own sin.
verses.little_bible: When people keep doing wrong, they get trapped — like a knot that ties itself tighter and tighter.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Doing wrong things ties us up like a knot. God's way sets us free.
verses.meaning: Sin has a way of trapping us — each wrong choice makes the next one easier and harder to escape. God's way leads to freedom.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way sets me free.
verses.prayer: God, when I am tempted to do wrong, help me remember it leads to a trap. Your way leads to freedom.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen how one small wrong choice leads to another, bigger one?
verses.family_discussion: How does following God keep us free instead of trapped?
verses.do_it_today: If there is a bad habit or wrong choice you keep making, tell God about it and ask Him to help you get free.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two paths: one leading to tangled ropes, one leading to wide open green fields with sunlight

verses.kjv: He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
verses.little_bible: Those who refuse to be taught will go astray — their own foolishness will lead them far from the right path.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Refusing to be taught leads you far from the right path.
verses.meaning: Rejecting instruction has a final, sad consequence. But this is a call to hope: we can choose the opposite — to receive instruction, walk wisely, and live well.
verses.memory_phrase: I choose to be taught and walk wisely.
verses.prayer: God, I choose to be taught by You. Lead me on the right path all my life.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between someone who is always learning and someone who refuses to learn?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family keep choosing to learn from God and from each other?
verses.do_it_today: As a family, choose one new thing to learn from God's Word this week — and commit to living by it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child turning back toward the right path, choosing wisdom, with God's light warmly welcoming them
